BeadValidator:\n97\t    \"\"\"\n98\t    Deterministic validator for escalate-generated bead bodies.\n99\t\n100\t    Enforces safety rules before bead creation via bead-forge CLI.\n101\t    \"\"\"\n102\t\n103\t    def __init__(self):\n104\t        self.rules = self._build_rules()\n105\t\n106\t    def _build_rules(self) -> list[ValidationRule]:\n107\t        \"\"\"Build validation rules.\"\"\"\n108\t        return [\n109\t            ValidationRule(\n110\t                rule_id=\"no_direct_kubectl_mutation\",\n111\t                name=\"No Direct kubectl Mutation\",\n112\t                description=\"Mutations must use GitOps (declarative-config), not direct kubectl\",\n113\t                severity=Severity.ERROR,\n114\t            ),\n115\t            ValidationRule(\n116\t                rule_id=\"gitops_required_for_mutations\",\n117\t                name=\"GitOps Required for Mutations\",\n118\t                description=\"Cluster changes must severity=Severity.ERROR,\n126\t            ),\n127\t            ValidationRule(\n128\t                rule_id=\"action_bead_requires_approval\",\n129\t                name=\"Action Bead Requires Approval\",\n130\t                description=\"Action-type beads require explicit user approval\",\n131\t                severity=Severity.WARNING,\n132\t                requires_approval=True,\n133\t            ),\n134\t        ]\n135\t\n136\t    def validate_bead_body(\n137\t        self,\n138\t        bead_body: str,\n139\t        bead_type: str = \"task\",\n140\t    ) -> ValidationResult:\n141\t        \"\"\"\n142\t        Validate a bead body deterministically.\n143\t\n144\t        Args:\n145\t            bead_body: The bead body markdown text\n146\t            bead_type: The bead type (task, action, self_modification, etc.)\n147\t\n148\t        Returns:\n149\t            ValidationResult with is_valid, requires_approval, violations\n150\t        \"\"\"\n151\t        logger.info(f\"Validating bead body (type={bead_type}, length={len(bead_body)})\")\n152\t\n153\t        # Normalize bead type\n154\t        try:\n155\t            bead_type_enum = BeadType(bead_type)\n156\t        except ValueError:\n157\t            # Default to TASK if unknown type\n158\t            bead_type_enum = BeadType.TASK\n159\t            logger.warning(f\"Unknown bead_type '{bead_type}', defaulting to TASK\")\n160\t\n161\t        # Lowercase for pattern matching\n162\t        body_lower = bead_body.lower()\n163\t\n164\t        # Check for information beads (no approval needed)\n165\t        is_informational = self._is_informational_bead(bead_body, body_lower)\n166\t        if is_informational and bead_type_enum == BeadType.TASK:\n167\t            logger.info(\"Bead appears to be informational - no approval required\")\n168\t            return ValidationResult.valid(BeadType.INFORMATIONAL)\n169\t\n170\t        violations = []\n171\t\n172\t        # Rule 1: Check for forbidden kubectl verbs\n173\t        kubectl_violations = self._check_forbidden_kubectl_verbs(bead_body, body_lower)\n174\t        violations.extend(kubectl_violations)\n175\t\n176\t        # Rule 2: If it's a mutation, check GitOps requirement\n177\t        # Exception: self_modification and monitoring_config beads are supposed to\n178\t        # modify prompts/configs directly, not through GitOps infrastructure workflow\n179\t        if self._is_mutation_bead(body_lower) and bead_type_enum not in (\n180\t            BeadType.SELF_MODIFICATION,\n181\t            BeadType.MONITORING_CONFIG,\n182\t        ):\n183\t            gitops_violations = self._check_gitops_requirement(bead_body, body_lower)\n184\t            violations.extend(gitops_violations)\n185\t\n186\t        # Rule 3: Check scoping requirements\n187\t        scoping_violations = self._check_scoping_requirement(bead_body, body_lower)\n188\t        violations.extend(scoping_violations)\n189\t\n190\t        # If there are ERROR-level violations, bead is invalid\n191\t        error_violations = [v for v in violations if v.severity == Severity.ERROR]\n192\t        if error_violations:\n193\t            hint = self._generate_reformulation_hint(error_violations)\n194\t            logger.warning(f\"Bead validation failed with {len(error_violations)} errors\")\n195\t            return ValidationResult.invalid(error_violations, reformulation_hint=hint)\n196\t\n197\t        # Rule 4: Action beads require approval\n198\t        if bead_type_enum == BeadType.ACTION:\n199\t            warning_violations = [v for v in violations if v.severity == Severity.WARNING]\n200\t            logger.info(f\"Action bead requires approval ({len(warning_violations)} warnings)\")\n201\t            return ValidationResult.requires_approval(\n202\t                bead_type=bead_type_enum,\n203\t                reason=\"Action-type beads require explicit user approval\",\n204\t                violations=warning_violations,\n205\t            )\n206\t\n207\t        # Self-modification and monitoring-config also require approval\n208\t        if bead_type_enum in (BeadType.SELF_MODIFICATION, BeadType.MONITORING_CONFIG):\n209\t            logger.info(f\"{bead_type_enum.value} bead requires approval\")\n210\t            return ValidationResult.requires_approval(\n211\t                bead_type=bead_type_enum,\n212\t                reason=f\"{bead_type_enum.value.replace('_', ' ').title()} beads require explicit user approval\",\n213\t            )\n214\t\n215\t        # Valid task/informational bead\n216\t        logger.info(\"Bead validation passed\")\n217\t        return ValidationResult.valid(bead_type_enum)\n218\t\n219\t    def _is_informational_bead(self, bead_body: str, body_lower: str) -> bool:\n220\t        \"\"\"Check if bead is informational (read-only operations).\"\"\"\n221\t        # Look for informational keywords in title/summary\n222\t        lines = bead_body.split(\"\\n\")\n223\t        first_few_lines = \"\\n\".join(lines[:5])\n224\t\n225\t        # Check for informational patterns\n226\t        for pattern in INFORMATIONAL_PATTERNS:\n227\t            if re.search(pattern, body_lower):\n228\t                # But make sure it's not a mutation in disguise\n229\t                if not any(verb in body_lower for verb in FORBIDDEN_KUBECTL_VERBS):\n230\t                    return True\n231\t\n232\t        return False\n233\t\n234\t    def _is_mutation_bead(self, body_lower: str) -> bool:\n235\t        \"\"\"Check if bead describes a mutation operation.\"\"\"\n236\t        # Check for any forbidden kubectl verb\n237\t        for verb in FORBIDDEN_KUBECTL_VERBS:\n238\t            if verb in body_lower:\n239\t                return True\n240\t\n241\t        # Check for mutation keywords\n242\t        mutation_keywords = [\"restart\", \"deploy\", \"scale\", \"delete\", \"update\", \"patch\"]\n243\t        for keyword in mutation_keywords:\n244\t            if keyword in body_lower:\n245\t                return True\n246\t\n247\t        return False\n248\t\n249\t    def _check_forbidden_kubectl_verbs(\n250\t        self,\n251\t        bead_body: str,\n252\t        body_lower: str,\n253\t    ) -> list[Violation]:\n254\t        \"\"\"Check for forbidden direct kubectl mutation commands.\"\"\"\n255\t        violations = []\n256\t\n257\t        for verb in FORBIDDEN_KUBECTL_VERBS:\n258\t            # Look for kubectl followed by forbidden verb\n259\t            pattern = rf\"\\bkubectl\\s+{re.escape(verb)}\\b\"\n260\t            matches = list(re.finditer(pattern, body_lower, re.MULTILINE))\n261\t\n262\t            for match in matches:\n263\t                # Extract line number\n264\t                line_num = body_lower[:match.start()].count(\"\\n\") + 1\n265\t                line_text = self._get_line_context(bead_body, line_num)\n266\t\n267\t                violations.append(Violation(\n268\t                    rule_id=\"no_direct_kubectl_mutation\",\n269\t                    severity=Severity.ERROR,\n270\t                    message=f\"Direct kubectl '{verb}' command detected. Mutations must use GitOps (declarative-config) approach.\",\n271\t                    line_number=line_num,\n272\t                    context=line_text.strip(),\n273\t                ))\n274\t\n275\t        return violations\n276\t\n277\t    def _check_gitops_requirement(\n278\t        self,\n279\t        bead_body: str,\n280\t        body_lower: str,\n281\t    ) -> list[Violation]:\n282\t        \"\"\"Check that mutations use GitOps (declarative-config) approach.\"\"\"\n283\t        violations = []\n284\t\n285\t        # If it's a mutation, check for GitOps patterns\n286\t        has_gitops = False\n287\t        for pattern in GITOPS_APPROVED_PATTERNS:\n288\t            if re.search(pattern, body_lower, re.IGNORECASE):\n289\t                has_gitops = True\n290\t                break\n291\t\n292\t        if not has_gitops:\n293\t            violations.append(Violation(\n294\t                rule_id=\"gitops_required_for_mutations\",\n295\t                severity=Severity.ERROR,\n296\t                message=\"Mutation operation detected but no GitOps pattern found. Cluster changes must go through declarative-config (jedarden/declarative-config), not direct kubectl.\",\n297\t            ))\n298\t\n299\t        return violations\n300\t\n301\t    def _check_scoping_requirement(\n302\t        self,\n303\t        bead_body: str,\n304\t        body_lower: str,\n305\t    ) -> list[Violation]:\n306\t        \"\"\"Check that commands include proper scoping.\"\"\"\n307\t        violations = []\n308\t\n309\t        has_scoping = False\n310\t        for pattern in SCOPING_PATTERNS:\n311\t            if re.search(pattern, body_lower, re.IGNORECASE):\n312\t                has_scoping = True\n313\t                break\n314\t\n315\t        # Only enforce scoping if it mentions kubectl or cluster operations\n316\t        if \"kubectl\" in body_lower or \"namespace\" in body_lower or \"cluster\" in body_lower:\n317\t            if not has_scoping:\n318\t                violations.append(Violation(\n319\t                    rule_id=\"scoping_required\",\n320\t                    severity=Severity.ERROR,\n321\t                    message=\"Command lacks proper scoping. Must include cluster, namespace, and/or resource scoping (e.g., 'namespace: production', 'cluster: ardenone-manager').\",\n322\t                ))\n323\t\n324\t        return violations\n325\t\n326\t    def _get_line_context(self, text: str, line_number: int, context_lines: int = 1) -> str:\n327\t        \"\"\"Get the context around a specific line.\"\"\"\n328\t        lines = text.split(\"\\n\")\n329\t        start = max(0, line_number - context_lines - 1)\n330\t        end = min(len(lines), line_number + context_lines)\n331\t        return \"\\n\".join(lines[start:end])\n332\t\n333\t    def _generate_reformulation_hint(self, violations: list[Violation]) -> str:\n334\t        \"\"\"Generate a hint for re-formulating the bead body.\"\"\"\n335\t        hints = []\n336\t\n337\t        for violation in violations:\n338\t            if violation.rule_id == \"no_direct_kubectl_mutation\":\n339\t                hints.append(\"Replace direct kubectl commands with GitOps approach: edit jedarden/declarative-config k8s/ files instead\")\n340\t            elif violation.rule_id == \"gitops_required_for_mutations\":\n341\t                hints.append(\"Use GitOps workflow: edit declarative-config → git commit → create/merge PR → ArgoCD sync\")\n342\t            elif violation.rule_id == \"scoping_required\":\n343\t                hints.append(\"Add explicit scoping: specify cluster (e.g., 'cluster: ardenone-manager'), namespace (e.g., 'namespace: production'), and target resources\")\n344\t\n345\t        if hints:\n346\t            return \"Re-formulation hint: \" + \"; \".join(hints)\n347\t        return \"Re-formulate the bead body to address the validation errors above.\"\n348\t\n349\t\n350\t# Global validator instance\n351\t_validator: Optional[BeadValidator] = None\n352\t\n353\t\n354\tdef get_validator() -> BeadValidator:\n355\t rule_id=\"no_direct_kubectl_mutation\",\n                name=\"No Direct kubectl Mutation\",\n                description=\"Mutations must use GitOps (declarative-config), not direct kubectl\",\n                severity=Severity.ERROR,\n            ),\n            ValidationRule(\n                rule_id=\"gitops_required_for_mutations\",\n                name=\"GitOps Required for Mutations\",\n rule_id=\"scoping_required\",\n                name=\"Scoping Required\",\n                description=\"Commands must include cluster/namespace/resource scoping\",\n    description=\"Action-type beads require explicit user approval\",\n                severity=Severity.WARNING,\n                requires_approval=True,\n            ),\n        ]\n\n    def validate_bead_body(\n        self,\n        bead_body: str,\n        bead_type: str = \"task\",\n    ) -> ValidationResult:\n        \"\"\"\n        Validate a bead body deterministically.\n\n        Args:\n            bead_body: The bead body markdown text\n            bead_type: The bead type (task, action, self_modification, etc.)\n\n        Returns:\n            ValidationResult with is_valid, requires_approval, violations\n        \"\"\"\n        logger.info(f\"Validating bead body (type={bead_type}, length={len(bead_body)})\")\n\n        # Normalize bead type\n        try:\n            bead_type_enum = BeadType(bead_type)\n        except ValueError:\n            # Default to TASK if unknown type\n            bead_type_enum = BeadType.TASK\n            logger.warning(f\"Unknown bead_type '{bead_type}', defaulting to TASK\")\n\n        # Lowercase for pattern matching\n        body_lower = bead_body.lower()\n\n        # Check for information beads (no approval needed)\n        is_informational = self._is_informational_bead(bead_body, body_lower)\n        if is_informational and bead_type_enum == BeadType.TASK:\n            logger.info(\"Bead appears to be informational - no approval required\")\n            return ValidationResult.valid(BeadType.INFORMATIONAL)\n\n        violations = []\n\n        # Rule 1: Check for forbidden kubectl verbs\n        kubectl_violations = self._check_forbidden_kubectl_verbs(bead_body, body_lower)\n        violations.extend(kubectl_violations)\n\n        # Rule 2: If it's a mutation, check GitOps requirement\n        # Exception: self_modification and monitoring_config beads are supposed to\n        # modify prompts/configs directly, not through GitOps infrastructure workflow\n        if self._is_mutation_bead(body_lower) and bead_type_enum not in (\n            BeadType.SELF_MODIFICATION,\n            BeadType.MONITORING_CONFIG,\n        ):\n            gitops_violations = self._check_gitops_requirement(bead_body,    violations.extend(scoping_violations)\n\n        # If there are ERROR-level violations, bead is invalid\n        error_violations = [v for v in violations if v.severity == Severity.ERROR]\n        if error_violations:\n            hint = self._generate_reformulation_hint(error_violations)\n            logger.warning(f\"Bead validation failed with {len(error_violations)} errors\")\n            return ValidationResult.invalid(error_violations, reformulation_hint=hint)\n\n        # Rule 4: Action beads require approval\n        if bead_type_enum == BeadType.ACTION:\n            warning_violations = [v for v in violations if v.severity == Severity.WARNING]\n            logger.info(f\"Action bead requires approval ({len(warning_violations)} warnings)\")\n            return ValidationResult.requires_approval(\n                bead_type=bead_type_enum,\n                reason=\"Action-type beads require explicit user approval\",\n                violations=warning_violations,\n            )\n\n        # Self-modification and monitoring-config also require approval\n        if bead_type_enum in (BeadType.SELF_MODIFICATION, BeadType.MONITORING_CONFIG):\n            logger.info(f\"{bead_type_enum.value} bead requires approval\")\n            return ValidationResult.requires_approval(\n                bead_type=bead_type_enum,\n                reason=f\"{bead_type_enum.value.replace('_', ' ').title()} beads require explicit user approval\",\n            )\n\n        # Valid task/informational \"\"\"Check if bead is informational (read-only operations).\"\"\"\n        # Look for informational keywords in title/summary\n        lines = bead_body.split(\"\\n\")\n        first_few_lines = \"\\n\".join(lines[:5])\n\n        # Check for informational patterns\n        for pattern in INFORMATIONAL_PATTERNS:\n            if re.search(pattern, body_lower):\n                # But make sure it's not a mutation in disguise\n                if not any(verb in body_lower for verb in FORBIDDEN_KUBECTL_VERBS):\n                    return True\n\n        return False\n\n    def _is_mutation_bead(self, body_lower: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if bead describes a mutation operation.\"\"\"\n        # Check for any forbidden kubectl verb\n        for verb in FORBIDDEN_KUBECTL_VERBS:\n            if verb in body_lower:\n                return True\n\n        # Check for mutation keywords\n        mutation_keywords = [\"restart\", \"deploy\", \"scale\", \"delete\", \"update\", \"patch\"]\n        for keyword in mutation_keywords:\n            if keyword in body_lower:\n                return True\n\n        return False\n\n    def _check_forbidden_kubectl_verbs(\n        self,\n        bead_body: str,\n        body_lower: str,\n    ) -> list[Violation]:\n        \"\"\"Check for forbidden direct kubectl mutation commands.\"\"\"\n        violations = []\n\n        for verb in FORBIDDEN_KUBECTL_VERBS:\n            # Look for kubectl followed by forbidden verb\n            pattern = rf\"\\bkubectl\\s+{re.escape(verb)}\\b\"\n            matches = list(re.finditer(pattern, body_lower, re.MULTILINE))\n\n            for match in matches:\n                # Extract line number\n                line_num = body_lower[:match.start()].count(\"\\n\") + 1\n                line_text = self._get_line_context(bead_body, line_num)\n\n                violations.append(Violation(\n                    rule_id=\"no_direct_kubectl_mutation\",\n                    severity=Severity.ERROR,\n                    message=f\"Direct kubectl '{verb}' command detected. Mutations must use GitOps (declarative-config) approach.\",\n                    line_number=line_num,\n                    context=line_text.strip(),\n                ))\n\n        return violations\n\n    def _check_gitops_requirement(\n        self,\n        bead_body: str,\n        body_lower: str,\n    ) -> list[Violation]:\n        \"\"\"Check that mutations use GitOps (declarative-config) approach.\"\"\"\n        violations = []\n\n        # If it's a mutation, check for GitOps patterns\n        has_gitops = False\n        for pattern in GITOPS_APPROVED_PATTERNS:\n            if re.search(pattern, body_lower, re.IGNORECASE):\n                has_gitops = True\n                break\n\n        if not has_gitops:\n            violations.append(Violation(\n                rule_id=\"gitops_required_for_mutations\",\n                severity=Severity.ERROR,\n                message=\"Mutation operation detected but no GitOps pattern found. Cluster changes must go through declarative-config (jedarden/declarative-config), not direct kubectl.\",\n            ))\n\n        return violations\n\n    def _check_scoping_requirement(\n        self,\n        bead_body: str,\n        body_lower: str,\n    ) -> list[Violation]:\n        \"\"\"Check that commands include proper scoping.\"\"\"\n        violations = []\n\n        has_scoping = False\n        for pattern in SCOPING_PATTERNS:\n            if re.search(pattern, body_lower, re.IGNORECASE):\n                has_scoping = True\n                break\n\n        # Only enforce scoping if it mentions kubectl or cluster operations\n        if \"kubectl\" in body_lower or \"namespace\" in body_lower or \"cluster\" in body_lower:\n            if not has_scoping:\n                violations.append(Violation(\n                    rule_id=\"scoping_required\",\n                    severity=Severity.ERROR,\n                    message=\"Command lacks proper scoping. Must include cluster, namespace, and/or resource scoping (e.g., 'namespace: production', 'cluster: ardenone-manager').\",\n                ))\n\n        return violations\n\n    def _get_line_context(self, text: str, line_number: int, context_lines: int = 1) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Get the context around a specific line.\"\"\"\n        lines = text.split(\"\\n\")\n        start = max(0, line_number - context_lines - 1)\n        end = min(len(lines), line_number + context_lines)\n        return \"\\n\".join(lines[start:end])\n\n    def _generate_reformulation_hint(self, violations: list[Violation]) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Generate a hint for re-formulating the bead body.\"\"\"\n        hints = []\n\n        for violation in violations:\n            if violation.rule_id == \"no_direct_kubectl_mutation\":\n                hints.append(\"Replace direct kubectl commands with GitOps approach: edit jedarden/declarative-config k8s/ files instead\")\n            elif violation.rule_id == \"gitops_required_for_mutations\":\n                hints.append(\"Use GitOps workflow: edit declarative-config → git commit → create/merge PR → ArgoCD sync\")\n            elif violation.rule_id == \"scoping_required\":\n                hints.append(\"Add explicit scoping: specify cluster (e.g., 'cluster: ardenone-manager'), namespace (e.g., 'namespace: production'), and target resources\")\n\n        if hints:\n            return \"Re-formulation hint: \" + \"; \".join(hints)\n        return \"Re-formulate the bead body to address the validation errors above.\"\n\n\n# Global validator instance\n_validator: Optional[BeadValidator] = None\n\n\ndef get_validator() -> BeadValidator:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global validator instance.\"\"\"\n    global _validator\n    if _validator is None:\n        _validator = BeadValidator()\n    return _validator\n","numLines":360,"startLine":1,"totalLines":360}}}

ArgoCD will sync\n248\t\"\"\"\n249\t\n250\t    call_count = 0\n251\t\n252\t    async def mock_call_simple(**kwargs):\n253\t        nonlocal call_count\n254\t        call_count += 1\n255\t        if call_count == 1:\n256\t            return invalid_body\n257\t        else:\n258\t            return valid_body\n259\t\n260\t    # If we get here, reformulation should have happened exactly once\n279\t                        assert call_count == 2, f\"Expected 2 LLM calls (original + 1 reformulation), got {call_count}\"\n280\t\n281\t                    except BeadApprovalRequired as e:\n282\t                        # =============================================================================\n614\t\n615\tdef test_kubectl_verb_detection_accuracy(validator):\n616\t    \"\"\"\n617\t    Test: Kubectl verb detection result.violations)\n628\t\n629\t\n630\tdef test_gitops_pattern_detection_accuracy(validator):\n631\t    \"\"\"\n632\t    Test: GitOps pattern detection is accurate.\n633\t\n634\t    Verify that
